perturbation.original_text: the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Material to any Third Party except with the Steering Committee’s prior written consent an d provided that no othe r legal disclosure requirement applies
perturbation.changed_text: the Receiving Party should endeavor not to disclose the Material to any Third Party, unless it seems reasonable, or with the Steering Committee’s consent.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not disclose' to 'should endeavor not to disclose' weakens the prohibition. Also, adding 'unless it seems reasonable' introduces a subjective criterion that can be interpreted broadly, potentially conflicting with data protection regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 5(1)(a) of GDPR requires that personal data be 'processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner'. Disclosing data based on what 'seems reasonable' introduces subjectivity and potential unfairness, contradicting the requirement for lawful and fair processing. Consent must also be explicit and informed; not inferred through 'reasonableness'.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: North Atlantic Treaty - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia (Redirected from Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty ) 1949 treaty forming the basis of NATO North Atlantic Treaty North Atlantic Treaty authentication page Type Military alliance Location Washington, D.C. Effective 24 August 1949 ; 75 years ago ( 1949-08-24 ) Condition Ratification by the majority of the signatories including Belgium, Canada, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, the United Kingdom, and the United States Parties 32 Albania Belgium Bulgaria Canada Croatia Czech Republic Denmark Estonia Finland France Germany Greece Hungary Iceland Italy Latvia Lithuania Luxembourg Montenegro Netherlands North Macedonia Norway Poland Portugal Romania Slovakia Slovenia Spain Sweden Turkey United Kingdom United States Depositary Government of the United States of America Languages English , French Full text North Atlantic Treaty at Wikisource The North Atlantic Treaty , also known as the Washington Treaty , [ 1 ] forms the legal basis of, and is implemented by, the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO). The treaty was signed in Washington, D.C. , on 4 April 1949. Background [ edit ] The treaty was signed in Washington, D.C., on 4 April 1949 by a committee which was chaired by US diplomat Theodore Achilles . Earlier secret talks had been held at the Pentagon between 22 March and 1 April 1948, of which Achilles said: The talks lasted about two weeks and by the time they finished, it had been secretly agreed that there would be a treaty, and I had a draft of one in the bottom drawer of my safe. It was never shown to anyone except Jack [ Hickerson ]. I wish I had kept it, but when I left the Department in 1950, I dutifully left it in the safe and I have never been able to trace it in the archives. It drew heavily on the Rio Treaty , and a bit of the Brussels Treaty , which had not yet been signed, but of which we were being kept heavily supplied with drafts. The eventual North Atlantic Treaty had the general form, and a good bit of the language of my first draft, but with a number of importan
perturbation.justification: The original contract uses 'shall not disclose,' which is a clear prohibition. The changed text weakens this prohibition to 'should endeavor not to disclose' and introduces a subjective criterion ('unless it seems reasonable'). This creates ambiguity regarding when disclosure is permissible, undermining the original obligation. The LLM explanation notes GDPR's requirement for lawful and fair data processing, and how 'reasonableness' introduces subjectivity that could contradict this. Although the scraped snippets aren't directly relevant, the change from a strict prohibition to a subjective allowance clearly weakens the legal obligation, thus making it more difficult to enforce the original agreement, and creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the ev ent of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Consortium Secretariat must be notified immediately by the Receiving Party, who shall cooperate fully in remedying same.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of loss or theft of any documents, items of work in progress, or any work products embodying Material, the Receiving Party should attempt to notify the Consortium Secretariat. The Receiving Party should consider cooperating in remedying same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must be notified immediately' to 'should attempt to notify' and 'shall cooperate fully' to 'should consider cooperating' weakens the obligation to report and remedy data breaches. It creates ambiguity and makes compliance discretionary rather than mandatory.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 33 of GDPR requires data controllers to notify the supervisory authority of a personal data breach 'without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it.' The modified text, which suggests only attempting to notify and considering cooperation, clearly contradicts this requirement for prompt and mandatory notification and full cooperation in remedying the breach.

Art. 33. Disposition guidance The President shall direct the Secretary of Defense to issue, in consultation with the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating when it is not operating as a service in the Navy, non-binding guidance regarding factors that commanders, convening authorities, staff judge advocates, and judge advoc
perturbation.justification: The change from 'must be notified immediately' to 'should attempt to notify' and 'shall cooperate fully' to 'should consider cooperating' weakens the mandatory nature of reporting and remediation, as stated in the original text. The Law Explanation points to GDPR Article 33, which requires notification of a personal data breach 'without undue delay'. While no GDPR snippet is provided, the original text imposes a clear obligation, and the modified text weakens that obligation significantly, making compliance discretionary. This creates ambiguity around a legally mandated action, contradicting the requirement for prompt notification.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall survive until the earlier of (i) such time as all the material disclosed hereunder becomes public ly known pursuant to Section 15 of the Consortium Agreement and made generally available without vi olation of this Agreement, or (i i) twelve (12) years from date of Registration.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall survive for a considerable amount of time, perhaps even until the material is publicly known or around twelve years.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'until the earlier of (i) such time as all the material disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known pursuant to Section 15 of the Consortium Agreement and made generally available without violation of this Agreement, or (i i) twelve (12) years from date of Registration' with 'for a considerable amount of time, perhaps even until the material is publicly known or around twelve years' makes the duration of the agreement vague and uncertain.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the Limitation Act 1980 sets limitation periods for simple contracts (6 years) and deeds (12 years). Specifying a 'considerable amount of time' or 'around twelve years' introduces uncertainty about the agreement's enforceability. Clear contract terms are required, which the modified version does not provide. It is vague, whereas the law demands set time parameters for when lawsuits can be commenced. This creates potential issues for determining when a claim can be brought.
perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies a clear timeline for the agreement's survival, based on either public disclosure or a fixed 12-year period. The changed text replaces this with 'a considerable amount of time, perhaps even until the material is publicly known or around twelve years,' which introduces unacceptable vagueness. While the Law Snippets are missing, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation indicate that this ambiguity creates uncertainty about the agreement's enforceability and potential limitation periods for bringing a lawsuit, therefore undermining the original contract's clarity and creates a legal contradiction.
